user_query:
Please set up a weekly automated update pipeline for our Financial Literacy and Inequality project that runs under a cron schedule and uses only the local inputs in input/. I need deterministic outputs saved under output/ each time it runs, plus a cron snippet that schedules the run. Do not use any external internet data or services.

Data sources (all required):
- input/credit_survey_2021.csv (respondent_id,income_quartile,literacy_score,state)
- input/credit_survey_2022.csv (respondent_id,income_quartile,literacy_score,state)
- input/program_participation_2022.csv (respondent_id,participated)
- input/inequality_metrics.csv (year,gini_coefficient,poverty_rate)
- input/grant_summary_draft.md (contains placeholders to replace)

Calculations to implement (round all reported numeric results to 2 decimals):
1) Overall mean literacy score for 2022 (from input/credit_survey_2022.csv).
2) Overall mean literacy score for 2021 (from input/credit_survey_2021.csv) and the year-over-year change = mean(2022) − mean(2021).
3) 2022 mean literacy score by income quartile (1–4) from input/credit_survey_2022.csv.
4) For 2022, mean literacy score for participants vs non-participants by joining input/credit_survey_2022.csv with input/program_participation_2022.csv on respondent_id; report the difference (participants minus non-participants).
5) The Gini coefficient for 2022 from input/inequality_metrics.csv.

Deliverables to create/update on each run:
- output/weekly_update.md — A concise status update that includes the following sections in this order: Overview; Key Metrics (list the four 2022 quartile means and the overall 2022 mean); Year-over-year change (state the 2021 mean and the 2022–2021 difference); Program impact snapshot (participants and non-participants means and their difference); Inequality context (state the 2022 Gini coefficient); Data sources (list the input files by path). All numbers must match the computed results.
- output/grant_summary_updated.md — Rewrite input/grant_summary_draft.md by replacing the placeholders {{LATEST_YEAR}}, {{MEAN_LATEST}}, {{YOY_CHANGE}}, {{GINI_LATEST}}, and {{PARTICIPANT_DIFF}} with the computed values (use 2022 as the latest year). Keep the original structure but ensure placeholders are replaced and the document reads cleanly.
- output/email_draft_to_team.txt — A draft message to my research team with the following fields in plain text: To: research-team@example.edu; Subject: Weekly automated update — Financial literacy and inequality; Body: a brief 1–2 paragraph summary that explicitly includes the 2022 overall mean, the 2021–2022 change, each quartile’s 2022 mean, the participants vs non-participants means and their difference, and the 2022 Gini; also mention that output/weekly_update.md and output/grant_summary_updated.md have been regenerated.
- output/schedule/weekly_finlit.cron — A single cron line that runs a local runner script every Monday at 07:15 (local time) and appends logs to output/logs/weekly_run.log. The cron command should invoke the runner at scripts/run_weekly_update.sh and redirect both stdout and stderr to output/logs/weekly_run.log.
- scripts/run_weekly_update.sh — A runnable script that, when executed, (re)generates all of the above outputs from the input files and writes a brief timestamped line to output/logs/weekly_run.log indicating the run completed.

Requirements and constraints:
- Use only the provided input files. Do not hardcode computed numbers; derive them from the CSVs on each run so that results can be recomputed for verification.
- Treat income_quartile as integers 1–4 and literacy_score as numeric. Round reported averages and differences to 2 decimals in all outputs.
- The cron line must reference scripts/run_weekly_update.sh and append to output/logs/weekly_run.log (e.g., using >> and 2>&1).
- Ensure all five deliverables are created under the specified paths.

Input paths (must be read):
- input/credit_survey_2021.csv
- input/credit_survey_2022.csv
- input/program_participation_2022.csv
- input/inequality_metrics.csv
- input/grant_summary_draft.md

Output paths (must be written):
- output/weekly_update.md
- output/grant_summary_updated.md
- output/email_draft_to_team.txt
- output/schedule/weekly_finlit.cron
- scripts/run_weekly_update.sh (executable) and output/logs/weekly_run.log

Success criteria:
- All required outputs exist, contain the correct computed values, and adhere to the section/field requirements.
- Placeholders in the updated grant summary are fully replaced with the correct values.
- The email draft includes each requested numeric result.
- The cron file contains exactly one line that schedules scripts/run_weekly_update.sh at 07:15 on Mondays and appends to output/logs/weekly_run.log.
- The solution is self-contained and executable locally without internet access. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
